title: Psychology and behaviour
explanation:
Psychology and behaviour

  • anxiety, motivation, perception, resilience, instinct
  • stimulus, behaviour, trait, coping, bias
  • verbs: perceive, motivate, cope, influence

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: High levels of ____ can affect sleep and concentration. | *anxiety | luggage | traffic
Her ____ helped her recover quickly from failure. | *resilience | receipt | platform
Some decisions are based on ____ rather than logic. | *instinct | agenda | deadline
A positive environment can increase student ____. | *motivation | compensation | customs

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Our ____ of colour can be affected by lighting. | perception
Loud noise acted as a powerful ____ in the experiment. | stimulus
She developed better ____ strategies during therapy. | coping
Advertisers try to ____ consumer choices. | influence
Confirmation ____ makes people favour information they already believe. | bias

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Aggressive ____ is often linked to stress. | *behaviour | furniture | platform
Honesty is considered an important personality ____. | *trait | ticket | recipe
Good leaders know how to ____ their teams. | *motivate | recycle | depart
Children ____ danger differently from adults. | *perceive | charge | queue
